sections.summary: Title only — document is the 'Standard for Danbo'.
Identifies the standard as CXS 264-1966 (formerly CODEX STAN C-3-1966), adopted 1966 and subsequently revised or amended in 2007, 2008, 2010, 2013, 2018, 2019 and 2022.

Describes the 2022 amendment replacing previous labelling provisions for non-retail containers with a requirement to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021) and shows the before-and-after text.
Scope: the standard applies to Danbo intended for direct consumption or for further processing in accordance with the product description in Section 2.

Defines Danbo as a ripened firm/semi-hard cheese with specified color, firm texture, pea-sized to up to 10 mm gas holes, flat squared or parallelepiped shape and optional smear-ripened rind, and states ripening to develop flavour and body is normally about three weeks at 12–20°C while allowing alternative ripening conditions or reduced ripening for product intended for further processing if similar properties are achieved.

Specifies essential composition and quality factors for Danbo: permitted raw materials (cow/buffalo milk and derivatives), allowed ingredients (starter cultures, coagulating enzymes, salts, water, ripening enzymes, processing aids and limited flours/starches for surface anti-caking on cut/sliced/shredded products), and compositional requirements including minimum milkfat-in-dry-matter (≥20%) with corresponding minimum dry matter levels by fat-in-dry-matter categories and reference ranges.
Permits cows' milk, buffaloes' milk, or mixtures and products derived from these milks as the raw materials for the product.

Lists permitted ingredients and processing aids for the cheese, including harmless starter cultures and other safe microbes, rennet/coagulating enzymes, sodium or potassium chloride as salt substitutes, potable water, safe ripening enzymes and processing aids, and allows rice/corn/potato flours or starches as surface anti-caking agents for cut/sliced/shredded products when used only as functionally necessary under GMP; includes an explanatory footnote on rindless cheese and ripening film.

Specifies compositional requirements: minimum milkfat in dry matter 20% (no maximum), reference fat-in-dry-matter range 45–55%, and a table linking fat-in-dry-matter ranges to corresponding minimum dry matter percentages (e.g., 20–<30% → 41% dry matter; ≥55% → 57%); any compositional modifications beyond the stated minima/maxima are non-compliant with Section 4.3.3 of CXS 206-1999.

Restricts food additive use to the functional classes justified in the accompanying table: permits certain colours and acidity regulators and preservatives in the cheese mass, permits some preservatives and anti-caking agents for surface/rind treatment, and limits acceptable additives to those listed in the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for the specified cheese categories.

Requires products to comply with Maximum Levels for contaminants and toxins specified in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and requires milk used for manufacture to comply with contaminant/toxin maxima and CAC maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides for milk.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with Codex hygiene texts (General Principles of Food Hygiene CXC 1-1969, Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products CXC 57-2004, and other relevant Codes) and compliance with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Introductory labelling provision referencing the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999); indicates that specific labelling provisions follow.

Allows use of the name 'Danbo' (and customary alternative spellings in the retail country) only if the cheese conforms to this Standard; requires prominent qualification on the name or label when fat content is outside the reference range but above the absolute minimum (expressed as fat in dry matter or percentage by mass), and permits the designation for products made from conforming cheese that are cut, sliced, shredded or grated.

Requires declaration of the country of origin defined as the country of manufacture, and when substantial transformation occurs in a second country that country is considered the origin for labelling.

Requires milk fat content to be declared in a manner acceptable in the country of retail sale, either as percentage by mass, as percentage of fat in dry matter, or as grams per serving (with number of servings stated).
Allows the date of manufacture to be declared instead of minimum durability information provided the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.
States that labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Mandates use of the methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions of this Standard for compliance checks.
